name: Lone Star Peanut Cookies - Country Living
recipeIngredient: Spanish peanuts, skinned and split into halves, or regular peanuts, quartered
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium-sized bowl, combine 1 1/2 cups flour, the baking powder, cinnamon, and salt; set aside.
2. In a large bowl, with an electric mixer on medium speed, beat sugar, 1/2 cup shortening, peanut butter, egg, and vanilla until smooth.
3. Reduce mixer speed to low; gradually beat in flour mixture until well blended. With a spoon, stir in enough of the remaining flour until stiff dough forms. Divide the dough into two halves.
4. Preheat oven to 375°F. Lightly grease 2 baking sheets or line with baking parchment paper.
5. On a lightly floured surface, roll out one dough half to 1/16-inch thickness. With a 3-1/4-inch star-shaped cookie cutter, cut the dough into stars. Place the dough stars, about 1/2 inch apart, on the greased sheet. Press a peanut half into the points of each star. Repeat with the other half of the dough.
6. Reroll all trimmings and cut out more stars.
7. Bake cookies 8 to 10 minutes or until golden brown. Let the cookies stand 1 minute on the sheet; cool completely on a wire rack.
8. In a microwave-safe cup, melt chocolate with the remaining tablespoon shortening in the microwave oven on high (100 percent) for 2 minutes. Stir until smooth, then transfer to a small waxed-paper cone or pastry bag fitted with a small writing tip. Write "Lone Star" or other words on each cookie. Let cookies stand until chocolate is set. Store cookies in an airtight container.
